steps.title: Graph the circle and the choices
steps.content: The fastest way is to graph the circle $(x+3)^2+(y-1)^2=25$ in Desmos and plot each answer choice. A point is in the interior if it lands inside the circle; it is not in the interior if it is on or outside the circle.

steps.title: Use the equation to verify the graph
steps.content: For a point to be inside the circle, $(x+3)^2+(y-1)^2<25$. Choice A gives $(-4)^2+2^2=20$, choice B gives $0$, and choice C gives $3^2+(-1)^2=10$; all three are less than $25$, so they are inside.

steps.title: Choose the outside point
steps.content: Choice D, $(3,2)$, gives $(3+3)^2+(2-1)^2=36+1=37$, which is greater than $25$. It is outside the circle, so it is not in the interior. Choice D is correct.
